title: Add header Cart button and basic /cart page

requirements.summary: Add an accessible Cart icon button in the SiteHeader top-right that remains usable on desktop and mobile without breaking the existing responsive nav/sheet behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop widths, a Cart icon/button is visible in the SiteHeader aligned to the right side of the header row.
The Cart option is accessible via keyboard navigation and includes an sr-only label like "Cart".
The existing desktop navigation links (Home/Products/Farms/About/Contact) continue to display and function as before.
On mobile widths, the header continues to show the menu toggle; Cart remains reachable (either as a visible icon in the header or as an item in the mobile sheet menu), and navigation remains usable.

file_operations.description: Add a top-right Cart icon button that navigates to "/cart" using TanStack Router Link, with an sr-only label for accessibility, ensuring it does not disrupt the current desktop nav or the mobile Sheet menu toggle. Use existing shadcn-ui Button/Sheet components for consistent styling and interaction; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing. Ensure Cart remains reachable on mobile (keep it visible in the header actions area and/or include it as an item inside the mobile Sheet nav).

requirements.summary: Create a basic Cart page and register a /cart route so the header Cart button navigates to a dedicated cart screen with an English empty-state message.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to "/cart" renders a Cart page without errors.
Clicking the header Cart option navigates to "/cart".
The Cart page shows a clear empty-state message in English (e.g., "Your cart is empty.") and fits the existing Farm2Retail visual styling.

file_operations.description: Create a Cart page component styled consistently with existing pages (use the standard container and typography patterns) and render an English empty-state message such as "Your cart is empty.". If using any shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card/Button), verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Register a new TanStack Router route for path "/cart" that renders CartPage, and wire it into the existing route tree so navigation from the header Cart button works as expected.
